description: Interactive simulation of NCERT Class 6 Science Chapter 8, Activity 8.2 — a tablespoon of water on a steel plate evaporates over time, letting students predict, observe, flip the plate to rule out seepage, and conclude that liquid water becomes water vapour.

initialMode.description: Starting mode: 'investigate' runs the predict/observe/flip/conclude experiment, 'explore' shows everyday evaporation examples

showModeSelector.description: Show/hide the Investigate / Everyday examples tab switcher in the header

enabledModes.description: Which modes appear in the mode selector

themeColor.description: Primary accent color (hex) used for buttons, progress bar, and highlights

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific dynamic content passed by the agent

totalMinutes.description: Total minutes the observation timeline represents, shown on the progress bar (0 min / midpoint / total) and as the elapsed-time chip

runSeconds.description: Real-world seconds the play/pause timer takes to animate from a full puddle to a completely dry plate

showVapour.description: Show the rising steam/vapour wisps above the puddle while the timer plays

initialPhase.description: Which step of the investigate flow to open on: Predict (make a guess), Observe (pour water, run the timer), Flip (check the underside for seepage), Conclude (summary of evaporation)

investigate.description: Step-by-step Activity 8.2 experiment: predict where the water goes, watch it evaporate from a poured puddle, flip the plate to confirm no seepage, then read the evaporation conclusion

explore.description: Tap through four everyday evaporation examples: drying puddles, wet clothes on a line, water on a hot pan, and hand sanitiser

predict.title: Make a prediction
predict.description: Student picks where they think the water will go (seep through, turn to vapour, vanish, or stay the same)

observe.title: Observe at regular intervals
observe.description: Spoon pours one tablespoon of water onto the plate; play the timer and watch the puddle shrink as vapour rises

flip.title: Did any water seep through?
flip.description: Flip the plate over to show the underside is completely dry, ruling out seepage through the steel

conclude.title: What we found out
conclude.description: Summary: steel is not porous, the water turned into water vapour, and this process is called evaporation
